rulings.Case Title: SIMA ZOKAEI VS. SALEM MANSOIR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Protective Order To Prevent Petitioners Order To Appear For Examination
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 6, 2023 line 1. RESPONDENT SALEM MANSOIR'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER TO PREVENT PETITIONER'S ORDER TO APPEAR FOR EXAMINATION is GRANTED. Opposing party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that CCP 917.1(d) does not apply to judgements entered on arbitration awards. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMESTOWN SA LLC; ET AL VS. JONATHAN DISHOTSKY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 6, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANT SDP PLUMBING, INC. DBA MR. ROOTER PLUMBING'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Triable issues of fact exist as to 1) whether Defendant Rooter made affirmative statements in the invoice; 2) whether Defendant Rooter concealed any information from anyone; 3) whether Defendant Rooter had reason to know that anyone would be misled; 4) whether Defendant Rooter made any misrepresentation to or concealed facts from anybody with the intention that they would rely on the same; 5) whether Plaintiffs relied on any affirmative statements or concealed facts from Defendant Rooter; and 6) whether Defendant Rooter acted with malice, oppression, or fraud. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice of the Third Amended Complaint and Answer to the Third Amended Complaint is Granted. (Evid. Code, Section 452(d).) Plaintiffs' Request for Judicial Notice of S.F. City and County Plumbing Permit Details Report is Granted. (Evid. Code, Section 452(h).) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MIKA ALICE RUEDA-ALBRIGHT ET AL VS. R&D CONSTRUCTION CO. INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 6, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC. AND PARAGON REAL ESTATE GROUP'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurer to the operative complaint based on the statute of limitations is overruled. The complaint provides a list of the undisclosed defects, some of which appear to have been latent defects that could have been observed (e.g. the shape of the walls, the large openings in the walls, etc.) in 2012 and some of which were not observed until the walls, floors and ceilings were opened during the renovation in 2021. The delayed discovery of those patent defects is sufficiently stated and demurrer cannot be sustained to a part of a cause of action. Therefore, the demurrer on these grounds is overruled. Demurrer is otherwise sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts (not conclusions) to support each element of each cause of action. These factual allegations should include (but are not limited to) the knowledge of defects by Ms. Muscat; the specifics of misrepresentations made (who, to whom, in what manner, the statement made) and facts supporting reliance on these misrepresentations; facts showing that Paragon and Compass knew or should have known about Ms. Muscat's misrepresentations, concealment, failure to disclose; the contractual provisions breached - Plaintiff may attach a copy of the contract and identify the breached paragraphs or quote the paragraphs in the body of the complaint. Opposing party failed to comply with SFRL 2.7B. Failure to comply in the future will result in motion being treated as unopposed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
